ety: in P. also sajāf, and zinjāf
def: s.f. Border (of a garment), fringe, edging, flounce.
adj. Having, or adorned with, a border; fringed.

s.m. Restraining; restraint, control; abstinence (from particular food on certain days), abstention; forbearance; sobriety; continence; the avoiding the infliction of pain (on others), humanity, compassionateness; — event, incident, occurrence
subs: v.t. To control (desire), to restrain, to keep in subjection, &c.

def: adj. & s.m. Restraining, curbing, controlling; abstemious, continent, temperate, sober; — one who subdues or controls his passions, a continent man, an ascetic, saint, sage, hermit.
s.m. = sājan, q.v.
s.m. = sahajnā, q.v.
part. Begotten, born, brought forth, produced, grown.
s.f. Evening, dusk, twilight
s.f. Evening twilight, evening ( = sāṅjh); — a particular evening prayer
subs: v.n. Evening twilight to come on
adv. Early in the evening ( = sar-ě-shām).

ety: sāṅjh, q.v.+Prk. अओ and इआ = S. अ+कः and इका
def: Images of cow-dung made by children during the dark half of the month Asin to represent idols; — hymns sung by Hindū girls in praise of Sānjhī.

ety: sāṅjh, q.v.+Prk. अओ and इआ = S. अ+कः and इका
def: Images of cow-dung made by children during the dark half of the month Asin to represent idols; — hymns sung by Hindū girls in praise of Sānjhī.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), The third (of four brothers, &c.); of the third rank or degree; — the third of four brothers.
s.m. Horse-radish ( = sahajnā, q.v.).

ety: perf. part. of sanjīdan, rt. sanj = Zend śać = S. शक्
def: part. adj. Weighed, measured; tried, proved, assayed; approved; composed; weighty, grave, serious; considerate.
s.f. Gravity, weight; solemnity, seriousness; considerateness.
adj. (f. -ikā), Living together; animating; — s.m. Name of a bull (in the Hitopadeśa).
s.m. Bringing to life, animating; re-animating, life-restoring; — the act of living together; — a cluster of four houses, a quadrangle; — name of one of the divisions of the infernal regions.
s.f. Making alive, causing life; restoring life; — a kind of elixir, elixir of life
subs: s.f. The art of vivifying or restoring to life.

def: adj. & s.m. (f. sanjīvinī and sanjīvanī), Rendering alive, vivifying, enlivening; restoring to life; re-animating; — one who or that which vivifies; restorer to life, re-animator.
v.t. = sājnā, q.v.

s.f. Sense, consciousness, knowledge, understanding, intellect, thought, mind; right mind; sign, token, symbol; gesture, gesticulation; name, appellation, title, designation, term, (in gram.) a noun substantive; a metaphor; an idiomatic phrase
subs: adj. Appertaining to a name or appellation, substantival
saṅjnā-vant (f. -vatī), adj. Recovered from unconsciousness or insensibility, become sensible, come to (one's) right mind, having consciousness; having a name or denomination.

def: adj. Knock-kneed.
adj. (f. -ā), Having a name, named, called.
part. Named, called, termed, designated.
adj. Knock-kneed.

ety: Prk. संजोगो; S. संयोगः, rt. युज् with सम्
def: s.m. Conjunction, connection, adherence, cohesion, union, junction, meeting, contact, intimate union, close connection, living together; agreement; combination, association; compounding; a kind of alliance in which two parties unite for a common object and attack with united power; — conjuncture, coincidence, event, occurrence, accident, opportunity, chance, hap, luck; — (in astron.) equinoctial point
subs: adv. By a coincidence, by chance, accidentally, fortuitously
(-kā), To join or unite (with), to combine (with), to associate (with), &c.

def: s.f. Wife of a sanjogī, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Connecting, joining, uniting; adjoining; — an ascetic who does not observe a vow of continence, but has a family; — a cage with two compartments for the male and female, a double cage.

ety: sanjo˚, &c. = S. संयोजय(ति), caus. of rt. युज् with सम्
def: v.t. To put together, to arrange, to prepare, make ready (cf. sajānā).

ety: sanjo˚, &c. = S. संयोजय(ति), caus. of rt. युज् with सम्
def: v.t. To put together, to arrange, to prepare, make ready (cf. sajānā).
s.m. Things arranged (on a tray, &c. for presentation).
s.m. = saṅyug, q.v.

ety: S. संयुक्तः, rt. युज् with सम्
def: part. adj. Connected, joined (with), united; compounded; blended, mixed; (in comp.) endowed with, possessed of.
s.m. Great heat or fever; heat (of fire, &c.); burning, scorching; a burn.
s.f. Fear, apprehension.

ety: prob. fr. S. श्वास; see sāṅs
def: s.f. Asthma.
s.f. (m. ?), A branch ( = P. shāḵẖ); — a lump of sweetmeat (particularly of jalebī).
s.m. = شنکو śanku, q.v.
s.f. Fear, apprehension; uncertainty, doubt, suspicion.
s.m. corr. of saṅkh, q.v.
s.m. Net-work of string, &c. (such as that which forms the bottom of a ćārpāʼī).
s.m. = sāṅkhā, sāṅkho, q.v.
s.f. Doubt, uncertainty, hesitation, scruple; distrust, suspicion, misgiving, fear, apprehension; presumption; hope; error
subs: v.n. To doubt, to hesitate; to be distrustful (of), to mistrust
adj. (f. -ī), Full of doubt or uncertainty, doubtful; fearful, afraid, apprehensive
adj. (f. -ā), Free from doubt, or apprehension; undoubting; fearless.

def: s.f. Fear, apprehension; uncertainty, doubt, suspicion.
s.f. A chain, &c. ( = sāṅkar, q.v.).
s.f. A chain, &c. ( = sāṅkal or sāṅkar, q.v.)
subs: s.m. Look and chain (as of a door).

def: s.m. Collection, accumulation, heap, quantity; (in Arith.) addition.
s.m. Heaping together, collecting, collection; uniting, blending; arranging; intermixture; contact, junction, collision; (in Arith.) addition, summation.
part. Heaped together, piled up, collected; blended, intermixed; arranged; added; — s.m. Arithmetic; addition.

s.m. Volition, will, wish, desire; mind, thought, idea; purpose, mental resolve or determination, resolution, vow; solemn vow to perform a ritual observance or work, declaration of purpose; bequest made by word of mouth, charitable donation; idea or expectation of advantage from any voluntary act
subs: s.m. A grant to a Brāhman
s.f. A dubious state of mind, hesitation
s.m. Deed of gift or assignment according to a previous vow.

def: part. Wished for, desired, aimed at, striven after; intended, purposed, determined, resolved on; conceived, imagined, thought, fancied, contrived.
v.n. To make a vow, form a resolution, &c.; — v.t. To vow; to dedicate, make a grant of; to give alms to (in fulfilment of a religious vow); to bequeath.
= saṅkārnā, q.v.
s.f. A chain; a door-chain; staple (of a lock); fetter; manacle; a kind of ornament worn by women, a twisted chain worn on the ankle.

ety: Prk. संकडं or संकडु; S. सङ्कटं
def: adj. Contracted, narrow, confined, close, strait, strict; tight, scant; — s.f. A narrow lane or passage; a strait; a defile; — difficulty, strait, trouble; a perplexing or embarrassing case.
s.m. Dust, sweepings, rubbish; the crackling of flame.
s.f. Beckoning, a sign, nod, wink; halloo.

s.m. Mixing together, intermixture, blending, confusing, confounding, confusion; irregular mixture, unlawful intermarriage, mixture of caste; a mixed caste or race, degraded caste (proceeding from the union of a man with a woman of a higher caste, or from the promiscuous intercourse of the four tribes, and again from the indiscriminate cohabitation of their descendants amongst each other. Most Hindūs of the present age are of one of the many branches of this race, the highest of which is impure, and is inferior to the Śūdra. According to the Rāmāyan, under the term saṅkar two kinds of men are included, 1˚ yonisaṅkar, 'mixed or degraded by birth'; 2˚ āćār-saṅkar, 'degraded by conduct').
s.m. = شنکر śaṅkar, q.v.
s.f. = شنکرا śaṅkarā, q.v.
s.f. A particular rāginī or musical mode.

r: śaṅkara, saṅkar, lit., śankarāćārya, śaṅkarābharuṅ, ˚ra, ābh˚
def: s.m. Name of a particular rāg or musical mode
subs: s.m. Name of a celebrated teacher of the Vedānta philosophy (he was a man of remarkable learning and sanctity; and is supposed to have lived between A.D. 650 and 740; but according to tradition he flourished 200 B.C.: he is said to have eradicated the Jain sect)
s.m. An epithet of Mahādev; an epithet of Śankarāćārya
vulg. sankar-bānī, s.f. The voice or utterance of Mahādev, a divine oracle.

def: s.f. Name of Pārvatī, the wife of Śiva.
v.t. & n. To beckon to; to nod, make a signal, wink, hint; to halloo; to inveigle.
v.n. = sakaṛnā or sukaṛnā, q.v.
s.m. Drawing together, contracting; drawing, attraction; making furrows, ploughing; — name of Baldev (brother of Krishṇ); name of one of the eleven Rudras.
adj. Similar, like, resembling.

adj. & s.m. Contracted, narrow, strait; crowded; impassable, impervious; — a narrow passage, a strait, defile; a strait, difficulty, trouble, vexation, anguish, pain, affliction, agony, pang, distress; misfortune; danger
subs: s.f. Name of a Hindū holiday in the month Māgh, sacred to Gaṇeś as the festival of his birth.

def: s.f. A skate fish.
s.m. corr. of śaṅkh-ćūṛ, q.v.s.v. śaṅkh.
s.m. Indication, sign, gesture, intimation, hint, allusion, innuendo; mark, token, symbol; gesticulation; — convention, agreement; engagement, appointment, assignation; condition, provision
subs: s.m. Appointed place, place of assignation
(-kā), To agree (to), to engage, stipulate; to provide (for)
vulg. saṅket-bāćak, s.m. (in Gram.), A demonstrative pronoun
s.m. Insinuation, hint, allusion, innuendo, double-entendre.

def: part. adj. Indicated; agreed upon, conventional; adapted, accommodated; non-natural.

s.m. A conch-shell (used by Hindūs as a vessel for offering libations, and also for blowing as a horn); a shell; &c. (see śaṅkh); — a particular high number, ten or a hundred billions; — adj. Artless, simple
subs: s.f. Name of a medicinal plant, Andropogon aciculatum?

def: s.m. A shell; the conch-shell (used by the Hindūs for offering libations, or when perforated at one end, for blowing as a horn); a horn, trumpet; a military drum; — a temporal bone, temple; the bone of the forehead, frontal bone; an elephant's cheek; a kind of perfume; a particular high number, ten or a hundred billions; — one of Kuvera's treasures
subs: (˚kha+as˚), s.m. The daitya or demon Saṇkha (slain by Vishṅu)
s.m. Name of a demon slain by Krishṇ
s.f. The sound of a conch or horn.

def: s.m. = sāṅkho, q.v.
s.f. corr. of sakhī, q.v.
s.m.(?) (in Garhwāl) A honeycomb.
s.f. = شنکهني śaṅkhinī, q.v.

s.f. Name of one of the four classes into which women are divided (described as tall and handsome, with long eyes and hair, marked with three lines on the neck, amorous and irascible, and neither stout nor thin — the worst description of woman, a very demon in look and in nature).
s.f.(m.?), A kind of poison, arsenic (syn. sammuʼl-fār).
s.m. A bridge, causeway, suspension-bridge (syn. pul).
s.m. The tree Shorea robusta, and its wood (syn. sāl, q.v.).

adj. Relating to number or calculation, numeral; enumerating; deliberating; reasoning; rational, discriminative; — s.m. One of the Hindū systems of philosophy (ascribed to Kapila); — (in Logic) reasoning, argument
subs: s.m. The Sānkhya doctrine or system of philosophy, or any treatise upon it

s.m. lit. 'Application of the Sānkhya doctrine to the knowledge of spirit,' name of the second chapter of the Bhagavad-gīta (supposed to be so named as setting forth the Sānkhya and Yoga doctrines in regard to the soul).

def: s.f. Reckoning up, numbering, calculation; muster; an account, a sum; a number, numeral; (in Gram.) number; — deliberation, reasoning, reflection; reason, intellect, understanding; manner
s.f. Numerableness, numeration, counting, number.
part. adj. To be reckoned or counted up; proper to be numbered or calculated; numerable, calculable.
part. adj. Mixed together, intermingled, confused, miscellaneous; crowded, closely packed; contracted, compressed, narrow, small; of mixed caste; impure, polluted, adulterated; spread, diffused.
s.m. (f. -ā), Greatly praising or celebrating, magnifying, extolling, lauding; glorification, honour, glory.
part. = شنکت śaṅkit, q.v.
part. Doubted; doubtful, uncertain; apprehended, feared; suspected; alarmed, frightened, apprehensive.

ety: saṅkal or sāṅkal, q.v.+Prk. इआ = S. इका
def: s.f. A small chain; a small chain (of silver, or other metal) worn on the ankle (by women).
s.m. = sāṅkho, q.v.

s.m. Contracting, shrivelling up; contraction; shrinking; fear, terror; reserve, diffidence, backwardness, shame, bashfulness; family honour; compression, abridgment, narrowness, diminution; shutting up, closing; — a sort of skate fish; — saffron.
s.m. A skate-fish, Raia sankur ( = śaṅku).
s.m. Contracting, shrinking, contraction; causing to shrink or close; astringing.
adj. & s.m. Shrinking, contracting, shrivelling up; retiring, shy, bashful, diffident; — a retiring or bashful man, &c.
adj. Contracted, shrunk, shrivelled; bashful, diffident, reserved, shy.
v.n. To shrink; — v.t. To cause to shrink; to make one ashamed, to abash.
adj. Contracted, narrow, cramped, &c.

ety: saṅkṛā(nā) + aʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. ता+इका
def: s.f. Contracting; crowding.

s.m. Difficult passage or progress, making (one's) way over difficult ground (as over rocks, or torrents, or inaccessible passes); the means of effecting a difficult passage, a causeway, a bridge; — a going, moving, travelling, traversing, passage from one point to another, progress; the passage of a planet through the zodiacal signs.
v.t. To cause to be contracted or narrowed, to cramp, &c. ( = sakṛānā, q.v.).
part. Met together, passed through or into; passed, gone; gone from one point to another, transferred; entered (into a new sign of the zodiac).
s.f. Going or meeting together, union; proceeding, procession, passage; passage of the sun or planetary bodies from one sign into another; name of a Hindū festival.
adj. Of or pertaining to the sankrānti.
s.f. Going or meeting together, union; proceeding, procession, passage; passage of the sun or planetary bodies from one sign into another; name of a Hindū festival.
part. = saṅkrānt, q.v.
s.f. = saṅkrānti, q.v.
s.f. = saṅkrānti, q.v.
s.f. A small chain, &c. (see sāṅkar).
s.f. (dialec.) = 1˚ śaṅkarī; — 2˚ sāṅkrī, qq.v.
s.m. Complete consumption, total destruction; loss, ruin, decline; the dissolution of all things, destruction of the world; end.

s.m. Throwing together; condensing; compression; conciseness, brevity; an abridgement, summary, abbreviation, abstract, compendium, epitome, précis, syllabus; — adj. Condensed, abridged; brief, concise
subs: (-kā, or -ko), To condense, to abridge, to epitomize, make a summary (of), &c.
saṅkshep yah or saṅkshep yah hai
The short (of it) is this; in short, in brief, in fine.

def: adj. & s.m. Abbreviating, abridging, reducing to a small compass; — an abridger, condenser, epitomizer, &c.
s.m. = sankshep, q.v.
part. Thrown together, heaped up; compressed, abridged, condensed, abbreviated; succinct, compendious; concise, compact, small.
s.f. Throwing together; compression; abridgment, brevity, conciseness; throwing, sending; transition; ambuscade.
s.m. Shaking about, trembling, agitation; disturbance; overturning, upsetting; pride, arrogance.
s.m. = شنکو śaṅku, q.v.

s.m. Fear, apprehension; — a stake, spike; pile, post, pale, pin, style, peg, pillar; a dart, javelin, spear; a weapon; the trunk (of a lopped tree); the gnomon (of a dial); needle (of a compass); a cross (used in surveying); — the tree Shorea robusta; the skate-fish, Raia sankur; a goose; an ant-hill; a very high number, ten billions (as implied by an innumerable collection of ants); a demon; poison; sin; an epithet of Śiva; a name of Kām-dev.
part. adj. Contracted, narrowed; shrivelled up, shrunk, wrinkled; unopened, closed, unblown (as a flower); abashed, backward, diffident.
adj. (f. -ā), Crowded together, thronged, mixed together, commingled; confused, perplexed, disordered; filled (with), full (of).
part. adj. Closely attached, stuck together, joined together, united (with), associated; sticking, adhering, adherent; fit, suitable, appropriate.
s.m. Friendly conversation, chat, discourse.
s.m. Lying down; — sleep.

ety: S. सम्मान, rt. मा with सम्
def: s.m. Measure.

ety: S. सम्मान, rt. मन् with सम्
def: s.m. Respect, regard, esteem, consideration, deference, veneration, reverence, honour, homage
v.t. = sanmān karnā, q.v.s.v. sanmān.
adj. & s.m. Respectful, deferential; reverential; civil, polite; — a respectful man, a polite man, &c.
part. adj. To be honoured, honourable, venerable, respectable, estimable.
s.m. = sannipāt, and sampāt, qq.v.
adj. = sammat, q.v.
s.m. corr. of samay, q.v.
s.m. (f. -ā), A sincere or true friend.
adj. (f. -ī), Opposite, &c.
subs: v.t. To bring (one) face to face (with, -ke), to confront
(-ke), To come in front (of), come face to face (with), to face.

ety: sān˚ = Prk. सण(इ) or सणे(इ) = S. सञ्जय(ति), caus. of rt. सञ्ज्; but cf. sauṅdnā
def: v.t. To impregnate (with); to rub in; to knead, mash, mix up (as flour, dough, earth, &c.); to rub, smear, stain, soil, defile; to implicate
see سننا sannā.
v.t. To whet, to sharpen.
adj. & s.m. Very skilful, expert, dexterous; industrious; — an expert manufacturer, a skilful workman.
v.n. To be impregnated; to be soiled, be stained, be smeared, be defiled; to be mixed up, be kneaded (as dough, flour, earth, &c.).
wety: Urdu verb set سُنْنا (sunnā) سُنانا (sunānā) سُنْوانا (sunvānā) Inherited from Sanskrit शृणोति (śṛṇoti), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *ćr̥náwti, from Proto-Indo-European *ḱl̥néwti, from *ḱlew- + *-né-.

def: part. (f. -ā), Tied together, bound, fastened, girt; armed, mailed, accoutred; harnessed; arranged, arrayed; prepared, ready; cruel, murderous; closely attached or connected, contiguous, bordering.
s.m. Accoutrements, armour, mail, coat of mail.
s.m. = sannāṭā, q.v.
v.n. = sansanānā, q.v.
s.m. = sanyāsī, q.v.

ety: S. सन्नर्द+कः, rt. नर्द् with सम्; Prk. prob. संनड्डओ
def: s.m. Loud or violent sound, rumbling noise, clatter (made by wind and rain or hail, &c. at a distance), howling (of the wind), roaring, roar (of waves, or a conflagration, &c.); violent blast or gust; a dashing or driving (of rain, &c.); ringing, whizzing, whiz (of bullets, &c.); vehemence, animation, briskness and eagerness; — a transport of passion or rage; — a howling wilderness, a dreary place or spot; a stunning blow or shock; a state of alarm or terror, consternation; amazement; anything monstrous or frightful; — adj. Enormous, huge, vast, monstrous; — still, silent, lonely, dreary, desolate, frightful
subs: (-ko), To receive a shock, to be shocked, be amazed, be terror-stricken, be stunned, to fall into a swoon
adj. (f. -ī), Violent, terrible, awful, fearful, monstrous, appalling, shocking; dreary, desolate
v.n. To fall into or continue in a state of alarm or terror, to be stricken motionless with terror or astonishment
v.n. To be or become motionless (with astonishment or fear, &c.), to be paralyzed (with fear, &c.).

def: s.f. Bowing down, reverential salutation, obeisance; reverence, humility; — sound, noise.
s.m. Juxtaposition, proximity, vicinity, &c. ( = san-nidhān).
s.m. Juxtaposition, proximity, nearness, vicinity; presence, appearance, perceptibility; placing down, depositing; receptacle, repository; receiving, taking charge (of).
s.f. = sannidhān, q.v.
s.m. Nearness, proximity, vicinity; presence, attendance.
part. Placed near together, near, contiguous, proximate, close at hand, present.
s.m. Bringing or drawing near; approximation; attraction; nearness, proximity, vicinity, presence; connection, relation.
adj. Near, close, contiguous, adjacent, proximate, approximate.

s.m. Flying down, alighting, descending, falling down; falling together, meeting, union, junction, mixture; contact, collision; collection, assemblage, multitude; miscellaneous collection; approach, arrival; — combined derangement of the three humours, a disease in which the body is seized with universal chilliness, deliquium.
adj. (f. -ā or-ī), Entering; sitting; collected, folded.
part. adj. (f. -ā), Entered together, met together, assembled, collected; stationed, encamped; stationed near, contiguous, neighbouring, near, at hand.

ety: rt. as, with ni and sam
def: s.m. Relinquishment, resignation, abandonment; abandonment of the world, profession of asceticism; the fourth religious order (āśram); giving up the body, sudden death; — deposit trust; stake, wager; — Indian spikenard.
s.m. Depositing, delivering over, entrusting to; resigning, relinquishing, abdication; abandonment of the world, renunciation of temporal concerns.
s.m. One who abandons or resigns; one who has abandoned all worldly possessions and affections, an ascetic, a devotee; a Brāhmaṇ of the fourth order or āśram, a religious mendicant.
s.m. corr. of sanmān, q.v.
s.m. A subdivision of the Brāhmaṇ class; an individual of that sub-division; — a thief, pilferer, robber.
adj. (f. -ī), Collected; ready; well, sound, in health
subs: v.t. To bring or get together, to collect.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Collected; ready; well, sound, in health
subs: v.t. To bring or get together, to collect.

def: s.m. Snake, serpent
subs: (or ḍartā hai), prov. 'One who has been bitten by a snake dreads a piece of rope'; a burnt child dreads the fire.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀲𑀧𑁆𑀧 (sappa), from Sanskrit सर्प (sarpá). Compare Punjabi سَپّ (sapp) and Sindhi سَپُ (sapu).

def: s.m. Mourning or chanting dirges (over the dead), mourning, wailing, weeping; a passing bell; — condolence
subs: v.n. To make a visit of condolence (to one), to condole (with)
(-kā), To mourn or bewail the death (of)
s.f. A suit of mourning; — holiday suit, best suit
s.m. A long mourning (so called because the Khatrī women of the Panjāb mourn for their dead for long periods).

def: s.m. (dialec.) = saperā, q.v.
v.n. = sapaṛnā, q.v.
s.m.(f. ?) = sanāpat, q.v.

r: saṅperā, saṅpolā, saṅpoliyā, sapolā, sapoliyā, saperā
def: = H سنپولا संपोला saṅpolā, = H سنپوليا संपोलिया saṅpoliyā, see sapolā; sapoliyā; and saperā.
s.f. A female snake; — a disorder which causes the hair to fall off; — a blemish in a horse
subs: s.f. A species of snake with two heads (the fem. is used for the male and female).

r: saṅpolā, saṅpoliyā, saṅperā, sapolā, sapoliyā, saperā
def: = H سنپوليا संपोलिया saṅpoliyā, = H سنپيرا संपेरा saṅperā, see sapolā; sapoliyā; and saperā.

r: saṅpoliyā, saṅpolā, saṅperā, sapolā, sapoliyā, saperā
def: = H سنپولا संपोला saṅpolā, = H سنپيرا संपेरा saṅperā, see sapolā; sapoliyā; and saperā.
s.m. = sāṅḍ, q.v.
s.m. Propitiating, appeasing, conciliation, propitiation; perfecting; perfect mediation.
s.m. Protecting, guarding, preserving, taking care of, saving; protection, charge, custody
subs: (˚ṇa+ar˚), adv. With the view of protecting or saving.

def: part. Protected, guarded, preserved, saved, taken care of.
s.m. A beginning; energy, zeal, impetuosity; agitation, flurry; anger, rage, wrath, fury; pride, arrogance.
adj. & s.m. Agitated, flurried; exasperated, angry, wrathful, furious; proud, arrogant; — a wrathful or furious man; a proud man; &c.
s.m. Sound, noise, clamour, uproar, tumult.
s.f. = sāṅḍnī, q.v.
s.m. Obstruction, opposition, impediment, hindrance, stop, prevention, check, curb; bond, fetter.
s.f. = saṇḍsī or saṇḍāsī, q.v.
s.f. Breath, breathing, respiration; sigh; — a unit of time (equal to six breathings); a crack or fissure (which lets in air)
subs: (or ulṭe sāṅs lenā), To draw in the breath, to gasp (as a person in agony)
To draw a deep breath; to heave a sigh, to sigh, to regret; to gasp, pant
To hold in (one's) breath, to pretend to be dead
(-kī), To be short of breath; to gasp, pant
(-kī), The breath to be stopped, to be suffocated, be stifled; to be smothered, be throttled
(-kī), To stop the breathing (of), to suffocate, stifle; to throttle
To take a long breath; to hold in the breath; to pretend to be dead
To take breath, to breathe, to draw breath; to sigh, to repine.
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi سانس (sans /⁠sā̃s⁠/) (c. 1565), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀲𑀸𑀲 (sāsa), from Sanskrit श्वास (śvāsá).

def: s.m. Doubt, misgiving, anxiety; fear, apprehension; imagination; reflection.
s.m. Doubt, &c. ( = saṅśay, q.v.).
part. adj. Stuck together, joined, attached, closely connected; close, contiguous; intent (on); endowed (with).
s.f. = sāṅsnī, q.v.
s.f. See s.v. san; — also = sansanāhaṭ, q.v.
v.n. = sansanānā; and sāṅsnā; qq.v.

s.f. Sounding, ringing, whistling, whizzing, whiz, soughing; singing (as a kettle), simmering; hissing, hiss; rustling, &c.; the sound which issues from a new earthen vessel on water being first poured into it.

ety: S. स्वन redupl.+ā = āw = Prk. āwe = S. āpi, caus. augment
def: v.n. To sound, resound, ring; to whistle (as a bullet), to whiz; to sough (as the wind); to sing (as a kettle); to simmer; to hiss; to rustle; to wheeze; to snuffle; to jingle, to tingle; to feel a tingling sensation; to thrill (with enjoyment, &c.), to be excited, to be beside oneself (with joy, or rage, &c.); to faint.

ety: sansan, q.v.+ī = Prk. इआ = S. इका
def: s.f. Tingling sensation.
s.m. Transmigration; the world, the universe; mankind; mundane existence; worldly interests or concerns; worldly illusion
s.m. = sansār, q.v.

s.m. Union, mixture, commixture, conjunction, intimate union, close contact, touch; proximity, contiguousness, approximation, association, society, intercourse, acquaintance, familiarity, intimate relation, consorting with, living together, meeting, interview; sexual connection, copulation, coition.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Mixed (with), united, connected, in contact (with); associated, keeping company, acquainted, friendly, familiar; — an associate, acquaintance, companion, a familiar friend.

adj. & s.m. Of or belonging to the world, worldly, mundane, lay, secular, temporal; mixing in society or in wordly affairs, engaged in worldly or secular occupations; — an animal, a sentient being, a man; — a worldly man; a man of business.
adj. Worldly, mundane ( = saṅsārī); pert.
adj. Belonging to the world, worldly, mundane; secular; lay
subs: (˚ka+up˚), adj. & s.m. Having recourse to worldly or secular measures; — one who has recourse to worldly contrivances, a time-server, temporizer; — worldly contrivance, temporizing, time-serving.

def: s.m. Punishment; pain ( = sāsat).
= H سنسي संसय saṅsay, saṅsai, (Braj) s.m. saṅsā and saṅśay, qq.v.
= H سنسو संसौ saṅsau s.m. saṅsā and saṅśay, qq.v.
s.m. Uncertainty, irresolution, doubt, hesitation, scruple, misgiving, suspicion, mistrust, apprehension, fear, anxiety, dread; risk, peril, danger
subs: (˚ya+āp˚), and saṅśayātmā (˚ya+āt˚), adj. & s.m. Having the mind beset with doubts, of an irresolute or hesitating disposition, doubtful, uncertain, dubious, hesitating, apprehensive, sceptical, mistrustful, diffident; — a doubter, sceptic; one who hesitates or wavers
(˚ya+av˚), s.f. State or condition of uncertainty, or of apprehension, &c.
s.m. One who removes doubt, or apprehension, or suspicion, &c.

def: adj. Disposed to doubt; feeling uncertain or irresolute; wavering, irresolute, uncertain, doubting, dubious, sceptical.
adj. Disposed to doubt; feeling uncertain or irresolute; wavering, irresolute, uncertain, doubting, dubious, sceptical.
adj. & s.m. Wavering, doubtful, &c. ( = saṅśayālu); — one who hesitates or wavers; a doubter, a sceptic.
adj. & s.m. Wavering, doubtful, &c. ( = saṅśayālu); — one who hesitates or wavers; a doubter, a sceptic.
adj. & s.m. Wavering, doubtful, &c. ( = saṅśayālu); — one who hesitates or wavers; a doubter, a sceptic.
adj. Fertile, productive, fruitful.
s.f. Pincers, forceps ( = saṇḍsī, q.v.); a screw-key, wrench, spanner.
s.m. A tribe of Indian gipsies; an individual of that tribe.

s.m. Making perfect, perfecting, completing, finishing, polishing, refining, perfection, refinement; institution; education; accomplishment; conception, idea, notion; impression, design; form, mould; the power of memory; self-reproductive quality; faculty, capacity, instinct, habit, passion; operation, influence; decoration, embellishment, ornament, elegance; making sacred, hallowing, purification, consecration, dedication; purity; a sanctifying or purificatory rite or essential ceremony; investiture with the sacred thread; obsequies; a marriage-ceremony.

part. & s.m. Carefully or accurately formed, artificially made, or produced, or constructed, artificial, elaborated, highly wrought, refined, polished, perfected, completed, finished; decorated, ornamented, embellished; excellent, best; cleansed, purified; consecrated, hallowed, sanctified; initiated; married; — language formed by accurate grammatical rules; refined, or polished, or highly-wrought speech; the sacred and classical language of the Hindūs
subs: (˚ta+anu˚), adj. & adv. Sanskritized; having relation to the Sanskrit language; in accordance with the Sanskrit; after the manner of the Sanskrit.

ety: sāṅs˚ = Prk. संस(इ) or संसे(इ) = S. स्त्रसय(ति), caus. of rt. स्त्रंस्
def: v.t. To chide, rebuke, threaten, snub; to punish; to distress.
v.n. = sāṅsnā, and sansanānā, qq.v.
s.f. A female of the gipsy tribe called sāṅsiyā, q.v.
s.m. Close or mutual contact, touch, conjunction, union, mixture; a laying hold of; perception, sense; the being touched, being affected; — adj. Contiguous, close; contagious.
s.m. Hearing, listening attentively; — promise, assent, agreement; — mixture.
s.m. Refuge, shelter, asylum; protection, patronage, favour; resting-place, dwelling-place; alliance.
s.f. Course, current, flow, stream; revolution; course of mundane existence, transmigration; the world.

s.m. A collection, aggregation, heap, quantity, crowd; the aggregation of primitive atoms, primary formation; conformation, configuration; form, figure, shape, fabrication, construction; common place of abode, vicinity, neighbourhood; a place where four roads meet; a place, station; position, situation; spot, mark, sign; standing still, stopping; dying, death.
s.f. (local) The season of spring; name of a month.

ety: Prk. संतो; S. सन्तस्, base सत्, part. of अस् 'to be'
def: adj. & s.m. Being, existing; essential; good, virtuous, pious; right, proper; excellent; steady, respectable, venerable; — a pious or venerable man, a saint; a good or simple man
subs: s.f. The state or condition of a saint, saintliness; venerableness
s.f., and sant-samāj, s.m. The assembly of the good (the term is applied chiefly to the Hindū ascetics regarded as to their corporate capacity)
s.m. The intercourse or union of the pious.

def: s.f. = شانتي śānti, q.v.

part. (f. -ā), Appeased, allayed, calmed, alleviated, pacified, hushed, calm, tranquil, free from passions, contented, undisturbed; meek, humbled; put a stop to, put an end to, destroyed, killed; deceased, dead; ceased; — s.m. One whose passions are subdued, an ascetic, one absorbed in meditation on the deity
subs: (˚ta+āt˚), adj. & s.m.f. Of a resigned or composed spirit, calm-minded, composed; — a person of a composed or resigned disposition
s.m. One whose fever is alleviated
s.m. (f. -ā), A person of subdued passions, or of tranquil disposition
v.t. To appease, allay, calm, assuage, &c. (see śānt).

def: part. corr. of شانت śānt, q.v.
s.f. Joining, sticking; contact, union, junction, cohesion; attachment, liaison; confederacy, collusion; — a knot; — a flail
subs: (or sāṭ-bāṭ), and sāṅṭ-gāṅṭ, s.f. Combining, leaguing, &c.; combination, league, confederacy, collusion, intrigue.

ety: sāṅṭ, q.v.+अओ = S. अ+कः; cf. soṅṭā
def: s.m. A stick or whip (for driving oxen, or for urging an elephant to fight); a goad; a spur; a sugar-cane.
adj. (f. -ī), Wicked, depraved; miserly.

ety: prob. fr. S. अस्यत्, part. of अस् 'to throw'
def: s.m. The piece of wood which is thrown in the game called geṛī, q.v.
s.m. 'The giver of happiness or joy to the virtuous,' an epithet of Vishṇu.

s.m. & f. = H سنتانا सन्ताना santānā, s.m. Extension, expansion, spreading; lineage, race, descent, family; offspring, progeny, children; a son or daughter; — one of the five trees of Indra's paradise.
s.f. The act of appeasing or reconciling, conciliation, reconcilement; palliation; mildness
subs: (-kī), To appease, to conciliate, to still, quiet, lull, comfort.

def: s.m. = S سنتان सन्तान san-tān, s.m. & f. Extension, expansion, spreading; lineage, race, descent, family; offspring, progeny, children; a son or daughter; — one of the five trees of Indra's paradise.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Of or relating to offspring; having offspring; — one who has offspring, a parent.
s.f. Froth, foam; cream; coagulated milk; custard; a cobweb; the blade of a knife or sword.
adj. Possessing a body, embodied.
s.m. Great heat, burning heat; inflammation; fire; affliction, pain, anguish, distress, sorrow, misery; passion; resentment; remorse, penitence, repentance; penance
subs: and santāp karnā (-kā), To inflict pain or misery (on), to afflict, &c.

def: adj. Made hot, heated, inflamed; consumed; tormented, pained, afflicted, distressed; sad, sorrowful; impassioned; penitent, suffering remorse; — santāpī, s.m. An afflicted person, &c.
adj. Made hot, heated, inflamed; consumed; tormented, pained, afflicted, distressed; sad, sorrowful; impassioned; penitent, suffering remorse; — santāpī, s.m. An afflicted person, &c.
part. Greatly heated or inflamed, red-hot, glowing; burnt, burnt up; vexed, inflamed with passion; suffering great pain or anguish, tormented, distressed, afflicted, pained, wretched.
s.m. corr. of sangtarā, q.v.
wety: Borrowed from Portuguese Sintra (“a town in Lisboa, Portugal”). Compare semantically Italian portogallo (“sweet orange”, literally “Portugal”) and Gujarati સંતરું (santrũ).

def: s.m. Crossing over, passing over; being saved.

s.f. Extent, expanse, width; continuity; a continuous line, row, range; uninterrupted succession, descent; lineage, race, offspring, posterity, progeny; a son, a daughter; a number of people, multitude.
part. Continued, continuous, uninterrupted, continual, lasting, perpetual, eternal.
s.f. = S شانتتو शान्तत्व śāntatva, s.m. Quietness, calmness, quietism; meekness.

s.f. Extent, expanse, width; continuity; a continuous line, row, range; uninterrupted succession, descent; lineage, race, offspring, posterity, progeny; a son, a daughter; a number of people, multitude.
s.m. = S شانتتا शान्तता śāntatā, s.f. Quietness, calmness, quietism; meekness.
adv. In the being; in the presence (of); during, whilst (syn. hote, or hote-huʼe, or huʼe: the word, in this sense, would appear to be peculiar to the Benares dialect as used by panḍits)
subs: ( = santī, q.v.), In the place (of, -ke) instead (of), in lieu (of), in substitution (for).

def: = H سانتي सान्ती sāntī, See santī, and sante.
s.f. = sāṅṭ, q.v.
adj. Wicked, depraved; roguish, crafty; miserly.
s.f. Reading, instruction, a lesson.
s.f. Thigh (syn. rān).
s.m. = sāṭhī, q.v.
v.t. = sāṅṭnā, q.v.
v.n. = saṭnā, q.v.
s.f. = sāthrī, q.v.
s.f. = شانتي śānti, q.v.
s.f. A flail; a small stick or goad, &c. (see sāṅṭā).
s.f. A succedaneum; a substitute; — postpn. Instead (of, -kī), &c. ( = sante, q.v.).

s.f. Quiet, quietness, tranquillity, calmness, peace, ease, comfort, rest, repose, remission; absence of passion, indifference to objects of pleasure or pain, stoicism; pacification, alleviation; consolation, &c.; cessation; preservation; pardon
subs: s.m. 'Tranquillizing section,' name of the twelfth book of the Mahā-bhārata

adj. & s.m. (f. -trī, and śānti-dāyak (f. -ikā) = śānti-dāʼī, adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Giving tranquillity, tranquillizing, imparting rest or repose, soothing, peace-giving; — a tranquillizer, comforter, &c.

def: = H سانتے सान्ते sānṭe, See santī, and sante.
s.f. A switch, a cane, a thin stick.
part. adj. Consoled, comforted, &c. (see śānt).

v.t. To stick, cause to adhere or cohere, to gum, paste; to join, unite; to combine; to join threads, to splice; to twist (rope, &c.); to weave; to attach (a person) to oneself, win (one) over or away (from)
s.m. = santosh, q.v.
adj. = santoshī, or santushṭ, q.v.
adj. = santoshī, or santushṭ, q.v.
s.m. Contentedness, content, satisfaction, gratification, pleasure, joy, delight, happiness; satiety; patience; — the thumb and forefinger
subs: (or santokh) karnā, To exercise contentedness, to be content; to practice patience, to be patient; to take comfort.

def: adj. = santushṭ, q.v.
part. adj. Satisfied (with, par), content, well-pleased, delighted, gratified; consoled, comforted, at rest; patient, resigned; at ease, comfortable.
s.f. Complete satisfaction, contentment, gratification ( = santosh).
s.f. Complete satisfaction, contentment, gratification ( = santosh).
s.m. Conciliation, reconcilement; mildness; appeasing; speaking kindly and in a conciliatory manner; friendly salutation and inquiry.
s.m. Level ground on the top or edge of a mountain, table-land; summit, ridge; top, end, point; precipice; a shoot, sprout; a forest, wood; a road; a gale of wind; a sage.
adv. Along with (one's) younger brother.
adj. Attended by favourable circumstances.
s.m. Tooth-powder.

adj. (in Gram.) Pronounced with a nasal sound, nasal (the term is applied to any letter that has anuswār or ćandra-vindu over it; also to the fifth letter of each of the first five classes of consonants in the Nāgarī).
s.m. = sān, 'whetstone'; — s.f. corr. of śānti; qq.v.
s.m. = sāmāk, q.v. (cf. sāmā).
s.m. Conversation, discourse; information, intelligence, news, report; history, story; communication (of news, &c.); assent, agreement, conformity, correspondence, sameness.
s.m. = sāmak and sāṅwā, qq.v.
adj. = next, q.v.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀲𑀸𑀫𑀮 (sāmala), from Sanskrit श्यामल (śyāmalá).

def: s.m. An epithet of Krishṇ ( = sāṅʼolī-ṣūrat).
s.m. = sāwan, q.v.
s.m. = sāmāk, q.v. (cf. sāmā).
s.f. = sāwanī, q.v.

ety: saṅwār˚ = Prk. संवारे(इ) = S. संवारय (ति), caus. of rt. सम्+वृ
def: v.t. To prepare, to construct; to dress, decorate, adorn, deck; to arrange, methodize, regulate, rectify, correct, mend, improve.
adj. & s.m. = sāwant, q.v.
s.f. Preparation, arrangement, regulation, rectification, correction, amendment, &c.
s.m. Restraint, self-control, forbearance, continence.

ety: saṅwār˚ = Prk. संवारे(इ) = S. संवारय (ति), caus. of rt. सम्+वृ
def: v.t. To prepare, to construct; to dress, decorate, adorn, deck; to arrange, methodize, regulate, rectify, correct, mend, improve.
v.n. To be prepared; to be dressed, or decorated, or decked; to be arranged, &c. (see saṅ wārnā); to suit; to prosper; to improve; to become steady; to recover, to come right again.
s.m. A year; an era; the era of Vikramāditya (reckoning from about 56¾ B.C.)
subs: (or sambat) bāṅdhnā, To establish an era; to mark or fix an epoch; to distinguish oneself by illustrious actions

def: s.f. A covered cart ( = sāṅgī, q.v.).
adj. (f. -ī), Of a dark or sallow complexion; dark, swarthy; sallow, brown, nut-brown; of handsome countenance; an epithet of Krishṇ
subs: adj. (f. -ī), Brown and salty; rich brown; — piquant, pleasing
adj. & s.m. Having a sallow or a handsome countenance; — one who has a sallow countenance, &c., an epithet of Krishṇ.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀲𑀸𑀫𑀮 (sāmala), from Sanskrit श्यामल (śyāmalá).

def: adj. = sāṅwlā, q.v.
